vitals.terrain: Road and gravel through national parks and mountains

course_description.overview: Non-stop ultracycling race from the Adriatic Sea at Silvi Paese through historical villages, National Parks, and amazing landscapes to the Simbruini Mountains.

key_segments.name: Start at Silvi Sea
key_segments.description: Departure from Adriatic coast through Abruzzo landscapes.

key_segments.name: National Parks and Villages
key_segments.description: Passing historical villages and national parks with scenic views.

key_segments.name: Simbruini Mountains Finish
key_segments.description: Climb to mountain finish after 4600m elevation gain.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Entry-level ultra designed for daytime riding without night sections, suitable for solo, pairs, or relay teams starting ultracycling.

logistics.getting_there: Start in Silvi Paese, Teramo; accessible by car or regional transport to Abruzzo coast.
logistics.accommodation: Pre-race check-in April 30-May 1; post-race celebration May 3; no specific accommodations listed.
logistics.registration_details: Opens Dec 1, 2025; closes April 7, 2026; includes race pack, GPS tracker rental, GPX files, medal, live tracking, media, briefing, and party.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road or gravel bike recommended; 50/34 chainset, 11/32 cassette, 25-22mm road wheels; GPS navigator required; lights and reflectives mandatory.
logistics.local_considerations: Open to traffic, follow Italian road rules; support car needs 2 crew with licenses; self-supported option available.

final_verdict.one_liner: Perfect entry to ultracycling: stunning Abruzzo day ride from sea to mountains.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: New ultra riders, pairs/teams avoiding night riding, scenery seekers.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for accessible challenge with high production and Italian landscapes.
pro_tips: Register early Dec 1, 2025 for best fees.
Prepare support crew with licenses and car.
Train for 4600m climb on road/gravel setup.
Use provided GPS tracker and navigator.
